gap_statement: Lack of reported psychometric validation (item-level difficulty/discrimination, reliability, reproducibility) for institutional pre-board/mock exams used as predictors in Philippine RT programs.
related_theme: Assessment design, psychometrics, and measurement issues
evidence_basis: Bera2024 outlines methods for item-level analysis, correction-for-guessing, and reproducibility metrics; synthesis notes that many institutional pre-board instruments lack such psychometric reporting. Rabinoa2025 used mock scores but did not report item-level psychometrics in supplied summary.
relevance_to_current_study: High — pre-board scores are a primary predictor; lack of psychometric validation risks misestimating predictive validity and making poor remediation decisions.
notes: Implement item analysis, reliability (e.g., Cronbach's alpha or equivalent), and consider corrected-for-guessing as sensitivity analyses before modeling.

gap_statement: Absence of large, multi-institutional, individual-level Philippine RT datasets linking GWA, professional-course grades, internship/practicum ratings, pre-board item-level scores, review participation, and RTLE subject-level scores.
related_theme: Population/context gap; Institutional and cohort/contextual predictors
evidence_basis: Garcia2026 provides multi-program discriminant analysis but not RT-specific multi-institutional RT-only datasets. Ibañez2025 offers aggregated institutional longitudinal analysis but not individual-level pooled records.
relevance_to_current_study: High — multi-institution pooling would increase generalizability but requires harmonized individual-level data and multi-level modeling.
notes: If only single-site data available, emphasize institution-specific scope and recommend multi-site replication.

gap_statement: Unclear operationalization and availability of RTLE subject-area scores and their linkage to institutional records for subject-level prediction.
related_theme: Pre-board / mock examination performance as predictor of licensure success
evidence_basis: Some studies report only pass/fail or overall scores; subject-area scores would allow more granular prediction but availability is uncertain in supplied evidence.
relevance_to_current_study: Moderate to high — subject-area RTLE scores would expand analytic possibilities (subject-specific predictors), but primary analyses can proceed with overall pass/fail if subject scores unavailable.
notes: Subject-area RTLE score availability remains To be confirmed, but the gap is supported by Stage 06 synthesis notes on outcome availability and linkage limits.

gap_statement: Inconsistent handling and reporting of repeat examinees (retakers) and missing cohort years in local retrospective studies; effect of inclusion/exclusion on predictive estimates is unclear.
evidence_basis: Flores2024 excluded retakers in dental sample, Cabatingan2024 notes missing years (2018, 2020), and Ibañez2025 highlights cohort-year variability — combined, these indicate inconsistency in handling retakers/missing years.
relevance_to_current_study: High — decisions about retakers and missing years can bias estimated predictor-outcome relationships; must be prespecified and sensitivity tested.
notes: Predefine inclusion/exclusion rules for retakers and plan sensitivity analyses including/excluding them; document missing-year handling (e.g., multiple imputation, cohort exclusion).

gap_statement: Limited evaluated evidence on the effectiveness of programmatic interventions (mock scheduling, peer mentoring, intensive review) proposed to improve RTLE outcomes in Philippine RT programs.
related_theme: Program policies, remediation, and review practices
evidence_basis: Rabinoa2025 and Gallardo2025 propose interventions based on correlational findings but do not present empirical evaluations; Gouveia2024 shows program-policy associations in a different jurisdiction with indirect mediation evidence.
relevance_to_current_study: Moderate — current retrospective study can identify at-risk indicators that inform interventions but cannot evaluate intervention effectiveness without prospective or quasi-experimental data.
notes: Frame recommendations as conditional and propose designs for prospective evaluation.

gap_statement: Ambiguity in institutional grading scales and whether higher numeric values consistently indicate better performance across datasets.
supporting_synthesis_source: synthesis_notes; Flores2024 example of sign interpretation
related_theme: Overall academic performance and professional-course grades as predictors
evidence_basis: Synthesized materials flag the need to confirm grade-scale directionality and conversion when combining or interpreting GWA and professional-course averages.
relevance_to_current_study: High — misinterpretation of grade direction can invert associations; confirm grade scale and transform consistently.
notes: Document grade-scale orientation and any transformations applied in analysis.

gap_statement: Lack of consistent application of item-level measurement adjustments (e.g., correction-for-guessing) and cognitive-level classification when using MCQ-based pre-board tests as predictors.
related_theme: Assessment design, psychometrics, and measurement issues
evidence_basis: Bera2024 documents correction-for-guessing and cognitive-level analyses as relevant practices; supplied RT studies rarely report such adjustments.
relevance_to_current_study: Moderate — applying these adjustments may change predictive performance and improve construct validity of pre-board scores.
notes: Plan sensitivity analyses using raw and corrected pre-board scores and report cognitive-level composition of item sets.

gap_statement: Unclear role and predictive value of non-cognitive measures (e.g., grit, self-reported preparedness) in Philippine RT licensure prediction due to small-sample and descriptive-only local studies.
evidence_basis: Ampaso2022 found null results for grit in a small RT sample; Gallardo2025 reports self-perceived readiness but no objective outcomes; Wolden2019 indicates small effect size in allied health but requires larger samples.
relevance_to_current_study: Low to moderate — non-cognitive measures may add incremental validity but require larger samples and concurrent objective linkage.
notes: Consider including non-cognitive measures only if reliable, validated, and available at scale; otherwise describe as contextual background.
